meaning: n.(住在日德兰半岛的古代日耳曼部落的)朱特人,朱特族,[植]黄麻
examples: It was so productive that there was a shortage of labour to harvest it, of bull carts to haul it to the threshing floor, of jute bags to store it.
A group of about 30 women are sitting on a large jute mat. They are all married.
Britain's textile industry has declined markedly especially in the cotton, jute and linen production.
Some jute producers have reneged on contracts to sell to the government, exacerbating shortages, Ms. Kanwar added.
Plans to expand a museum celebrating Dundee's jute industry have received preliminary backing from the Heritage Lottery Fund.
